questions.question: Explain the difference between 'break' and 'continue' statements in Java loops with code examples.
keyPoints: break causes loop to exit immediately
Both affect only the innermost loop unless labeled

questions.question: Write a Java code snippet using an enhanced for-each loop to iterate over a List of Strings and print each element in uppercase.
keyPoints: Enhanced for-each syntax: for(Type var : collection)

questions.question: How would you use a labeled break to exit from a nested loop in Java? Explain with an example.
keyPoints: Exits outer loop from inner loop

questions.question: Write a Servlet code fragment that iterates over all HTTP request parameter names and prints both parameter name and its value.
keyPoints: Proper null checks for parameter values

questions.question: Compare 'while' and 'do-while' loops in Java, explaining when each would be preferred.
keyPoints: While is pre-test (may execute zero times)
Do-while is post-test (guarantees at least one execution)
Use do-while for menu-driven programs needing at least one selection

questions.question: Write a JSP code snippet that uses a for loop to generate an HTML table with 5 rows and 3 columns, displaying row and column numbers in each cell.
keyPoints: Nested loops for rows and columns
Inner loop runs (n-i) times for each i

questions.question: Design a complete Servlet that processes a multi-valued HTML form parameter (e.g., 'selectedCourses' with checkboxes) and displays the selected courses in an HTML list. Include proper HTML output formatting.
keyPoints: Extend HttpServlet, override doGet or doPost

questions.question: Write a Java program that demonstrates the use of labeled continue to skip iterations in nested loops. Create a scenario where you need to find pairs (i, j) where both i and j are even numbers from 1 to 10, using labeled continue to skip pairs where either number is odd.
keyPoints: Show how it skips to next iteration of outer loop
Demonstrate output with only even-even pairs

questions.question: Compare and contrast the three types of loops in Java (for, while, do-while) with respect to their use cases, syntax, and behavior in different scenarios. Provide practical examples including when each should be used in Servlet/JSP development.
keyPoints: Do-while: at least one execution needed
Servlet: for-each over parameters, while for enumeration
JSP: for loops for dynamic table generation
